mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in non-farm payrolls indicates a robust labor market, which supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns. This mechanism suggests that a healthy employment environment is a fundamental driver of equity market performance.
evidence_refs: fact:Historically, strong employment correlates with economic growth.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS growth is positive but S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in more than 55% of observations over a 5-year rolling window, this hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) exceeds a 4% year-over-year threshold, it signals an overheating economy, prompting the Federal Reserve to tighten monetary policy. This tightening typically leads to higher discount rates and reduced corporate profitability expectations, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Central banks typically raise rates to combat high inflation.
failure_condition: If CPIAUCSL YoY growth exceeds 4% but S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances over a 3-year rolling window, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of strong retail sales (RSAFS) and low federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS) indicates robust consumer demand supported by accommodative monetary conditions. This environment fosters corporate revenue growth and lower borrowing costs, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns as investor confidence rises.
evidence_refs: fact:Low interest rates generally stimulate economic activity.
failure_condition: If periods of strong RSAFS growth and low FEDFUNDS coincide, but S&P 500 20-day returns are consistently negative (Rank IC < 0.05) over a 4-year period, this hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) by more than 0.5 percentage points over three months signals a deteriorating labor market and potential economic recession. This leads to reduced consumer spending and corporate profits, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns as investors price in a downturn.
evidence_refs: fact:Recessions are typically preceded by rising unemployment.
failure_condition: If UNRATE rises by more than 0.5pp in 3 months, but S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 40% of such instances over a 10-year period, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts (HOUST) below a critical level of 1.5 million units (annualized) indicates a significant slowdown in the housing market, which often precedes broader economic weakness. This weakness translates into reduced demand and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing market health is a key economic indicator.
failure_condition: If HOUST falls below 1500k units, but S&P 500 5-day returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances over a 7-year rolling window, this hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in industrial production (INDPRO) signifies a healthy manufacturing sector and robust supply-side capacity, which underpins overall economic expansion. This positive economic backdrop supports corporate earnings and investor sentiment, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Industrial output is a key measure of economic activity.
failure_condition: If INDPRO shows positive month-over-month growth, but S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in more than 60% of observations over a 5-year rolling window, this hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of strong real GDP growth (GDPC1) and a low unemployment rate (UNRATE) indicates a robust and efficient economy operating near full capacity. This environment is highly conducive to corporate profit growth and investor confidence, driving strong positive S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Strong GDP and low unemployment are hallmarks of economic prosperity.
failure_condition: If GDPC1 YoY growth exceeds 2% and UNRATE is below 5%, but S&P 500 60-day returns are negative in more than 45% of such periods over a 10-year rolling window, this hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) rises above 5%, it signals a restrictive monetary policy stance aimed at curbing inflation or cooling an overheating economy. Such high rates increase borrowing costs for corporations and consumers, dampening economic activity and leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:High interest rates typically slow economic growth.
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS exceeds 5.0%, but S&P 500 5-day returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances over a 5-year rolling window, this hypothesis is falsified.
